- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_site tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_site PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_site)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
  icount_spin

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip harness: QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
#
# Memory between QEMU and vsim is shared via an /dev/shm-backed mmap
# (128 MB). Works for both asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_*) and
# C-level fixtures (rt_c_*). C fixtures require shared mmap so the
# stack/heap survives handoffs; asm fixtures don't need it but run
# fine with it.
#
# Env:
#   FIXTURE         REQUIRED. .elf name under hybrid/test/ (no .elf suffix).
#   EXPECT_RC       Final exit code to assert (default 0).
#   TRIGGER_MODE    csr | pc (default csr).
#   TIMEOUT         vsim leg timeout in seconds (default 60).
#   CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1   assert mcycle/minstret strictly increased
#                              between QEMU 1's drain and vsim's drain.
#   VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM  override built paths.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

# HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN when it was sourced first).
if [ -z "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ] && [ -f "$TOP_ROOT/config.env" ]; then
  . "$TOP_ROOT/config.env"
fi

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:?FIXTURE env var is required (e.g. FIXTURE=rt_c_hello)}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"

    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in

content.content:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
#
# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want
# to bypass the env var.

TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)
ifeq ($(strip $(TOOLCHAIN)),)
$(error HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN is not set. Run `source ./setup.sh` from the repo root, or pass `TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain` on the command line)
endif
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump demo

all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Note: handoff.elf, handoff_exit.elf, spin.elf are legacy (referenced only
# by EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL handoff_smoke.cpp). They still build for completeness
# but are not in active CTest. All targets now share test.ld at 0x00100000.

# Switching-mode demo runner: builds the fixtures if needed, then walks
# T1..T7 from $(DEMO_DIR), printing a banner per mode and a final
# pass/fail tally. Honors FILTER=<regex> to subset the run.
demo: all
	@bash demo.sh

spin.elf: spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

# CSR-less spin for F5 plugin-icount e2e. Linked at 0x00100000 (test.ld)
# so andes_ae350's MROM at 0x80000000 stays clear when loaded via -bios.
icount_spin.elf: icount_spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c icount_spin.S -o icount_spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) icount_spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_exit.o -o $@

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
#
# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want
# to bypass the env var.

TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)
ifeq ($(strip $(TOOLCHAIN)),)
$(error HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN is not set. Run `source ./setup.sh` from the repo root, or pass `TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain` on the command line)
endif
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

input.description: Look at how e2e CTest registration uses fixtures

content.content:
add_dependencies(check test_icount_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # End-to-end round-trip tests. Each entry registers two CTest cases
  # (csr + pc triggers) on the unified e2e.sh harness, which sets up an
  # /dev/shm-backed mmap and runs QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
  # The harness skips (return 77) when artifacts are missing, so the
  # same CTestTestfile works whether ctest is invoked from the
  # container's /work/build or from the host's build/.
  #
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set
  # in e2e.sh); the *_matmul fixtures need ~120s because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim before drain.
  set(VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES
    # Asm fixtures (no C runtime). Shared mmap is not strictly required
    # for these but the unified harness uses it anyway -- the ~20 ms
    # mktemp cost is negligible vs the ~15-20s per-test wall.
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/
    # libgloss/handler). The C stack and heap must survive the
    # vsim -> QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the
    # kernel (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd); QEMU2 stores y and
    # scalar-verifies. V regfile transport rides on the save-all /
    # reload-all helpers in runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c}.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in the
    # vsim phase, verifies in QEMU2. Exercises Tier-A+B restore.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar recompute under QEMU 2.
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the tail
      # short. Use the per-fixture timeout as the cost so long fixtures
      # (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )

content.text:
Yes, possible. Here's the lay of the land.

**What is cross-cutting (QEMU + vsim) today:**

| Location | Content | Move? |
|---|---|---|
| `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/` | RISC-V ELF fixtures: `*.S`, `*.c`, `Makefile`, `test.ld`, `runtime/` | Yes |
| `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` | The 3-phase harness (QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2) | Yes |
| `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_*.sh` | Negative drivers (corrupt_state, gdbstub_timeout, no_qemu_binary, pty_handback) | Yes |
| `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_plugin_*.sh`, `probe_vcsr_drain.sh` | Plugin-only shell tests (no vsim) | Yes (even more so) |
| `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh` | Switching-mode demos T1..T7 | Yes |
| `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (e2e block) | CTest registration of all `VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES` | Drop or shim |
| `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.test.cpp` | doctest unit tests using `recording_bus.hpp` duck-types | **Stay** - they're hybrid-layer unit tests; depend on vsim's `vsim_add_test` + build wiring |

**Proposed top-level layout after move:**
```
hybrid_sim/
  tests/
    e2e.sh                   <- moved from hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/
    drivers/rt_corrupt_state.sh, rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh, ...
    plugin/test_plugin_*.sh, probe_vcsr_drain.sh
    demos/demo_*.sh
    fixtures/                <- moved from hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/
      Makefile, test.ld, *.S, *.c
      runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, v_regfile_xfer.*, rt_c_helpers.h, ...}
  run_e2e.sh                 <- already top-level; rewire paths
```

**The gotchas - things that need real work, not just `git mv`:**

1. **CTest in submodule becomes stale.** `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` registers `test_e2e_*` against `../tests/hybrid/e2e.sh`. Either:
   - drop the e2e block from `HybridConfig.cmake` and rely solely on top-level `run_e2e.sh`, OR
   - keep it but point at `$ENV{HYBRID_SIM_ROOT}/tests/e2e.sh` (the submodule must not assume parent layout, so this needs an env-var contract).

2. **Path assumptions in `e2e.sh`** (`REPO_ROOT="$SCRIPT_DIR/../.."`, `VSIM_BIN`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_ELF`) need rewiring once it's no longer inside the submodule.

3. **`hybrid_vsim` submodule's own CI** probably runs these e2e tests today. After the move, the submodule's CI can't run them standalone unless you either (a) accept that e2e validation only happens at the parent repo, or (b) have the submodule's CI clone the parent.

4. **`runtime/` headers** - check whether `core_v5.h`, `memory_map.h`, `platform.h` are copied from vsim's CPU description or are standalone. If they mirror vsim internals, the move adds a sync burden.

5. **Demo scripts** call out to fixture paths and may have other coupling to vsim's directory layout - need scrubbing.

Want me to spell out a concrete migration plan (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR with the structural git-mv as one commit and behavioral rewiring as separate commits), or scope it down (e.g., move only fixtures + `e2e.sh`, leave the unit `.test.cpp` files where they are)?

# Migration plan: cross-cutting e2e tests -> top-level `hybrid_sim/`

## Invariant (enforced after every phase)
`JOBS=8 MODE=both bash run_e2e.sh` produces the same green test list as the Phase-0 baseline. Any divergence blocks the next phase.

## Phase 0 - Baseline (no commits)
```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash run_e2e.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-baseline.log
grep '^\[ok\]'   /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | sort > /tmp/baseline-pass.txt
grep '^\[FAIL\]' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | sort > /tmp/baseline-fail.txt
```
These two files are the diff target for every later phase.

---

## Phase 1 - Lay down the new tree (structural, top-level repo)
Copy, do NOT delete the originals yet. Both layouts coexist until Phase 5.

Target:
```
hybrid_sim/tests/
  e2e.sh                <- cp hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh
  fixtures/             <- cp -r hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/   (Makefile, *.S, *.c, runtime/, test.ld)
  drivers/              <- cp hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,gdbstub_timeout,no_qemu_binary,pty_handback}.sh
  plugin/               <- cp test_plugin_*.sh probe_vcsr_drain.sh qemu_silent_stub.py
  demos/                <- cp demo_*.sh
```
- Commit: `structural: copy e2e harness + fixtures to top-level tests/`
- **Verify**: `run_e2e.sh` still uses old paths -> baseline pass list unchanged.

---

## Phase 2 - Rewire path resolution in the new copies (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR, top-level repo)

### 2a. RED - one failing smoke test
Write `tests/smoke_e2e_paths.sh` that invokes the new `tests/e2e.sh` with `FIXTURE=rt_c_hello TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Run it; it fails because `e2e.sh` resolves `REPO_ROOT="$SCRIPT_DIR/../.."` which lands on `hybrid_sim/` instead of `hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/`, so `VSIM_BIN`/`VSIM_PLUGIN`/`VSIM_ELF` paths are wrong.
- Commit: `behavioral: add failing smoke test for top-level tests/e2e.sh`

### 2b. GREEN - minimal path edits in `tests/e2e.sh`
Change exactly these lines:
| Old (was inside submodule)                              | New (top-level)                                |
|---------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/../..`                           | `TOP_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..`                      |
| `VSIM_BIN=$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium`         | `$TOP_ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` |
| `VSIM_PLUGIN=$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/...`         | `$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`   |
| `VSIM_ELF=$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/$FIXTURE.elf`          | `$TOP_ROOT/tests/fixtures/$FIXTURE.elf`        |

Smoke test passes.
- Commit: `behavioral: resolve harness paths from top-level tests/ root`

### 2c. REFACTOR - extract path block to `tests/lib/paths.sh`
The same resolution logic is duplicated across `e2e.sh`, the negative `rt_*.sh` drivers, and the demo scripts. Pull into one sourced helper. Baseline still green.
- Commit: `structural: dedupe harness path resolution into tests/lib/paths.sh`

### 2d. Drivers + plugin + demos
Repeat 2a/2b minimally per file family. Each family = one RED test + one GREEN patch + one REFACTOR (use the helper from 2c). Commits split by family:
- `behavioral: rewire negative drivers (drivers/rt_*.sh) to top-level paths`
- `behavioral: rewire plugin-only shell tests to top-level paths`
- `behavioral: rewire switching-mode demo scripts to top-level paths`

---

## Phase 3 - Point `run_e2e.sh` at the new tree (behavioral, top-level repo)
Two-line change at the top of the script:
```
HARNESS="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid"   ->  "$ROOT/tests"
TEST_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test"   ->  "$ROOT/tests/fixtures"
```
Plus the fixture-build call: `make -C "$TEST_DIR"`.

Run `JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh`, diff against `/tmp/baseline-pass.txt` - must match exactly.
- Commit: `behavioral: point run_e2e.sh at top-level tests/ tree`

---

## Phase 4 - Drop the e2e CTest block from the submodule (structural, hybrid_vsim repo)
In `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`, delete the `if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID) ... foreach(entry ${VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES}) ...` block. **Keep** all `vsim_add_test(test_*` doctest registrations - those are hybrid-layer unit tests and they stay.
- Verify inside submodule: `ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-unit` runs the same doctest set as before; no `test_e2e_*` entries remain.
- Commit (in submodule): `structural: drop e2e CTest registration (moved to parent)`

---

## Phase 5 - Delete originals from submodule (structural, hybrid_vsim repo)
Split by file family so each commit reverts cleanly:
- `git rm -r hybrid/test`  -> `structural: remove e2e fixtures (moved to parent)`
- `git rm tests/hybrid/{e2e.sh,rt_*.sh,test_plugin_*.sh,probe_vcsr_drain.sh,demo_*.sh,qemu_silent_stub.py}` -> `structural: remove e2e shell drivers (moved to parent)`

After each: `ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-unit` still green.

---

## Phase 6 - Submodule pointer bump (structural, top-level repo)
```
git -C hybrid_vsim checkout <HEAD-after-phase-5>
git add hybrid_vsim
git commit -m "structural: bump hybrid_vsim after e2e relocation"
```
Verify: `JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh` matches `/tmp/baseline-pass.txt`. The submodule no longer contains the e2e tree.

---

## Phase 7 - Docs (structural, top-level repo)
Update only the files that reference the old paths:
- `CLAUDE.md` - "Key Files" table (`tests/e2e.sh`, `tests/fixtures/Makefile`, ...) and the "Test Fixtures" section path
- `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` - fixture-build invocation, e2e invocation
- `README.md` - any path references
- `ROADMAP.md` / `TEST_ROADMAP.md` - mark this migration as done

Commit: `structural: docs: reflect relocated e2e tree`

---

## Compound-effect payoff
- New fixture = 1 commit in `hybrid_sim/`, no submodule pointer bump in 3 places
- e2e harness changes don't leak to other `hybrid_vsim` consumers (e.g. the standalone vsim demo)
- `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (top-level) and `tests/fixtures/` (top-level) become co-located - the wire format and the fixtures that exercise it live together

## Risks / decisions needed before starting
1. **History preservation** - cross-repo `cp` loses per-file `git blame` in the new home. If blame matters, use `git filter-repo --path hybrid/test --path tests/hybrid` on a clone of `hybrid_vsim` and merge with `--allow-unrelated-histories` in Phase 1. Adds half a day. Defer unless you actually use blame in this tree.
2. **Submodule CI** - any pipeline in `hybrid_vsim` that runs `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` silently passes with 0 tests after Phase 4. Need to either delete that job or have it clone the parent repo.
3. **`runtime/` headers** - verify before Phase 1 that `core_v5.h`, `memory_map.h`, `platform.h` are standalone fixture support files, not sync-mirrors of vsim CPU-description files. If they mirror, they keep a sync burden.

---

## Suggested execution cadence
Each phase is one session (per your "one feature per session" rule). Order is strict for phases 1-6; Phase 7 (docs) can land anywhere after Phase 6.

Want me to:
- (a) start with Phase 0 + Phase 1 now, or
- (b) first answer the three risk items above (history, CI, runtime headers) so the plan is locked, or
- (c) write this plan to `specs/e2e_migration.md` and add the 7 phases to `ROADMAP.md` as trackable features?

# ROADMAP: gem5 CPU-Switch Parity

Tracks 12 independent features to extend hybrid_sim (QEMU + vsim) with the four
CPU-switch mechanisms from `specs/hybrid-sim-gem5-switch-parity.md`. One
session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: behavioural+structural commits landed and tests pass

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 ----+----> F4
F2 ----+
F3 ----+
F5 ----+----> F6
       +----> F7 ----> F12 ----> F11
       +----> F8 ----> F9 ----> F10
```

Migration order: ship F1, F2, F3 first (infrastructure layer). After that,
F5/F6/F7 parallelise across sessions because they touch independent files.
F12 (SimPoint binary integration) sits between BBV emission (F7) and
slice-consume (F11) because F11's slice indices come from SimPoint's
clustering output.

## Features

### F1 - Rebrand plugin drain-exit code 0 -> 200

- Status: done
- Type: structural
- Description: Today `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261` exits 0 on drain;
  workload completion and drain are indistinguishable. vsim already exits
  200 (`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp:7`). Bring the plugin in
  line. The orchestrator (F2+) relies on this distinction.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261`
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` (exit-code assertions)
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh`
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: a test in `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh` runs
  the plugin with a CSR fixture and asserts QEMU exits with code 200.
  Fails on today's 0.

### F2 - Orchestrator skeleton (Python package)

- Status: done
- Type: structural (new code)
- Description: Create `tools/orchestrator/` Python package. CLI parsing,
  plan loading from YAML, dry-run that prints the planned spawns. No real
  spawn yet.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/__init__.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/plan.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` (new)
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_plan_parses.py` loads
  `examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` and asserts the resulting `Session` has
  three `Step` entries. Fails because no parser exists.

### F3 - ctypes ABI binding + state-file validator

- Status: done
- Type: structural (new code)
- Description: Mirror `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`'s `hybrid_state_v1` in
  Python via ctypes. Field-offset asserts at import time. Add
  `validate_state_file(path)` checking magic 0x42594841, version 1,
  exact `sizeof(hybrid_state_v1)` bytes. Wire into orchestrator as the
  post-spawn gate.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/abi.py` (new)
  - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (read-only)
  - `include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h` (read-only; mirror its asserts)
- Dependencies: F2

# ROADMAP: Relocate cross-cutting e2e tests to top-level `hybrid_sim/`

Tracks the migration of the e2e test tree (fixtures, harness, drivers, plugin
shell tests, demos) out of the `hybrid_vsim/` submodule and into the top-level
`hybrid_sim/` repo, so the wire format (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) and the
tests that exercise it live in the same repo.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: structural+behavioral commits landed and Phase-0 baseline pass list matches

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash run_e2e.sh
# pass list == /tmp/baseline-pass.txt from F0
```

Any divergence blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F0 ----> F1 ----> F2 ----> F3 ----> F4 ----> F5 ----> F6 ----> F7
                  (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR
                   sub-loop inside F2)
```

Strict serial order from F1..F6. F7 (docs) can land any time after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (answer before F1)

1. **History preservation** - cross-repo `cp` loses per-file `git blame` in the
   new home. If blame matters, do F1 via `git filter-repo --path hybrid/test
   --path tests/hybrid` on a clone of `hybrid_vsim` and merge with
   `--allow-unrelated-histories`. Adds ~half a day. Default: plain copy,
   accept that blame for moved files starts fresh in `hybrid_sim/`.
2. **Submodule CI** - any pipeline in `hybrid_vsim` that runs
   `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` silently passes with 0 tests after F4. Decide:
   delete the CI job, or have it clone the parent repo.
3. **`runtime/` headers** - confirm `core_v5.h`, `memory_map.h`, `platform.h`
   under `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/` are standalone fixture files, not
   sync-mirrors of vsim CPU description headers. If they mirror, add a
   regenerate step or keep them in the submodule.

## Features

### F0 - Capture baseline pass list

- Status: not-started
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Snapshot the current green test list so every later phase can
  diff against it. Runs both CSR and PC trigger modes.
- Steps:
  ```
  JOBS=8 MODE=both bash run_e2e.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-baseline.log
  grep '^\[ok\]'   /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | sort > /tmp/baseline-pass.txt
  grep '^\[FAIL\]' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | sort > /tmp/baseline-fail.txt
  ```

- Dependencies: none

  coexist after this commit. Old path is still authoritative.

  ```
  hybrid_sim/tests/
    e2e.sh           <- cp hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh
    fixtures/        <- cp -r hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/       (Makefile, *.S, *.c, runtime/, test.ld, semihost_exit.inc)
    drivers/         <- cp hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,gdbstub_timeout,no_qemu_binary,pty_handback}.sh
    plugin/          <- cp test_plugin_*.sh probe_vcsr_drain.sh qemu_silent_stub.py

-### Test Fixture Tiers
+### Test Fixtures
 
-| Tier | Harness | Memory model | Examples |
-|------|---------|-------------|---------|
-| 1 | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` | QEMU-private RAM | handoff_roundtrip, rt_all_gprs, rt_csr_survival |

+All fixtures run on the unified `tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` harness, which
+sets up an `/dev/shm`-backed shared mmap (128 MB) so memory survives
+the QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 round-trip. Two fixture families:
 
-Tier-2 is required for C-level fixtures because the C stack and heap must survive the vsim -> QEMU2 boundary. The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`, then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.
+| Family   | Examples                                                    | Notes |
+|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
+| asm      | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem` | no C runtime; don't strictly need shared mmap but use it anyway (~20 ms cost is negligible). |

+
+The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`,
+then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.
 
 Fixture source lives in `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/`. C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).

+|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
+| asm      | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem` | no C runtime; don't strictly need shared mmap but use it anyway (~20 ms cost is negligible). |

+
+The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`,
+then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.

C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).

behavioral: unify roundtrip_e2e.sh + rt_shared_mem.sh into tests/hybrid/e2e.sh

Both legacy harnesses ran a QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 round-trip; the only
real distinction was that rt_shared_mem.sh allocated /dev/shm and passed
--shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file. The unified e2e.sh sets
up the shared mmap unconditionally (asm fixtures pay ~20 ms which is
negligible against the ~15-20s per-test wall) so one driver covers every
fixture family.

CTest registration in cmake/HybridConfig.cmake collapses the two
foreach blocks (VSIM_RT_FIXTURES + VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES) into a single
VSIM_E2E_FIXTURES list. Demo wrappers (demo_csr_oneshot.sh,
demo_pc_oneshot.sh) updated to call e2e.sh. Three design docs
(hybrid_plan.md, hybrid_vsim.md, test_plan.md) updated to the new name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

1	# ROADMAP: Relocate cross-cutting e2e tests to top-level `hybrid_sim/`
2	
3	Tracks the migration of the e2e test tree (fixtures, harness, drivers, plugin
4	shell tests, demos) out of the `verilator/` submodule and into the top-level
5	`hybrid_sim/` repo, so the wire format (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) and the
6	tests that exercise it live in the same repo.
7	
8	One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
9	completion (done).
10	
11	## Status Legend
12	
13	- not-started: feature has not begun
14	- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
15	- done: structural+behavioral commits landed and Phase-0 baseline pass list matches
16	
17	## Global Invariant
18	
19	After every feature lands, this must hold:
20	
21	```
22	JOBS=8 MODE=both bash run_e2e.sh
23	# pass list == /tmp/baseline-pass.txt from F0
24	```
25	
26	Any divergence blocks the next feature.
27	
28	## Dependency DAG
29	
30	```
31	F0 ----> F1 ----> F2 ----> F3 ----> F4 ----> F5 ----> F6 ----> F7
32	                  (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR
33	                   sub-loop inside F2)
34	```
35	
36	Strict serial order from F1..F6. F7 (docs) can land any time after F6.
37	
38	## Pre-flight decisions (answer before F1)
39	
40	1. **History preservation** - cross-repo `cp` loses per-file `git blame` in the
41	   new home. If blame matters, do F1 via `git filter-repo --path hybrid/test
42	   --path tests/hybrid` on a clone of `verilator` and merge with
43	   `--allow-unrelated-histories`. Adds ~half a day. Default: plain copy,
44	   accept that blame for moved files starts fresh in `hybrid_sim/`.
45	2. **Submodule CI** - any pipeline in `verilator` that runs
46	   `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` silently passes with 0 tests after F4. Decide:
47	   delete the CI job, or have it clone the parent repo.
48	3. **`runtime/` headers** - confirm `core_v5.h`, `memory_map.h`, `platform.h`
49	   under `verilator/hybrid/test/runtime/` are standalone fixture files, not
50	   sync-mirrors of vsim CPU description headers. If they mirror, add a
51	   regenerate step or keep them in the submodule.
52	
53	## Features
54	
55	### F0 - Capture baseline pass list
56	
57	- Status: not-started
58	- Type: no commit (safety net)
59	- Description: Snapshot the current green test list so every later phase can
60	  diff against it. Runs both CSR and PC trigger modes.
61	- Steps:
62	  ```
63	  JOBS=8 MODE=both bash run_e2e.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-baseline.log
64	  grep '^\[ok\]'   /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | sort > /tmp/baseline-pass.txt
65	  grep '^\[FAIL\]' /tmp/e2e-baseline.log | sort > /tmp/baseline-fail.txt
66	  ```
67	- Output: `/tmp/baseline-pass.txt` is the diff target for F1..F6.
68	- Dependencies: none
69	- TDD RED: n/a (no code change).
70	
71	### F1 - Lay down top-level `tests/` tree (copy-only)
72	
73	- Status: not-started
74	- Type: structural (top-level repo)
75	- Description: Copy the e2e tree into `hybrid_sim/tests/` WITHOUT touching the
76	  originals in the submodule and WITHOUT touching `run_e2e.sh`. Both layouts
77	  coexist after this commit. Old path is still authoritative.
78	- Target layout:
79	  ```
80	  hybrid_sim/tests/
81	    e2e.sh           <- cp verilator/tests/hybrid/e2e.sh
82	    fixtures/        <- cp -r verilator/hybrid/test/       (Makefile, *.S, *.c, runtime/, test.ld, semihost_exit.inc)
83	    drivers/         <- cp verilator/tests/hybrid/rt_{corrupt_state,gdbstub_timeout,no_qemu_binary,pty_handback}.sh
84	    plugin/          <- cp test_plugin_*.sh probe_vcsr_drain.sh qemu_silent_stub.py
85	    demos/           <- cp demo_*.sh

91	  - `tests/plugin/*.sh` (new)

93	- Dependencies: F0

98	### F2 - Rewire path resolution in new copies (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR)
